principles.cultural_memory_over_pixels: Color identity is determined by collective cultural memory and cinematographic intent, not by pixel dominance or frame frequency.
principles.one_film_one_color: A film can never be canonically associated with more than one primary color. The color identity is singular and defining.
principles.ai_informs_editors_decide: AI analysis provides reasoning and validation, but editorial judgment always has final authority over color assignments.
principles.monochrome_is_editorial: Monochromatic mode assignment (claroscuro_dramatico vs monocromatico_intimo) is an editorial decision based on cinematographic style, not chromatic analysis.
principles.examples_are_illustrative: All film examples in this doctrine are illustrative references only. They are non-exclusive, non-canonical, and not assignments. No film may appear as a reference example for more than one color.

colors.name: Rojo Pasional
colors.cinematographic_notes: Associated with intense emotional states, bloodshed, desire, and moments of dramatic revelation

colors.name: Naranja Apocalíptico
colors.cinematographic_notes: Explosive energy, desert heat, civilizational collapse, kinetic warfare

colors.name: Ámbar Desértico
colors.cinematographic_notes: Golden hour light in arid landscapes, temporal distance, weathered textures

colors.name: Amarillo Lúdico
colors.cinematographic_notes: Bright, saturated palettes expressing joy, curiosity, and youthful energy

colors.name: Verde Lima
colors.cinematographic_notes: Organic, sun-dappled environments, pastoral beauty, life force

colors.name: Verde Esmeralda
colors.cinematographic_notes: Rich, saturated greens suggesting luxury, obsession, or concealed truth

colors.name: Verde Distópico
colors.cultural_context: Control systems, night vision, industrial fear
colors.cinematographic_notes: Sickly, artificial greens associated with technological dread and systemic control

colors.name: Cian Melancólico
colors.cinematographic_notes: Cool, desaturated blues expressing isolation and quiet introspection

colors.name: Azul Nocturno
colors.cinematographic_notes: Deep, saturated blues of artificial night, rain-slicked streets, existential searching

colors.name: Violeta Cinético
colors.cinematographic_notes: Vibrant, aggressive purples expressing heightened states and sensory overload

colors.name: Púrpura Onírico
colors.cinematographic_notes: Soft, suffused purples suggesting reverie, nostalgia, or liminal spaces

colors.name: Magenta Pop
colors.cinematographic_notes: Bold, saturated magentas expressing defiance, spectacle, and cultural commentary

colors.name: Blanco Polar
colors.cultural_context: Moral void, institutional coldness, existential emptiness, winter isolation
colors.cinematographic_notes: Cold white environments suggesting absence, clinical detachment, or blankness of extreme moral situations

colors.name: Negro Abismo
colors.cultural_context: Gotham darkness, gothic menace, total visual void, noir extremity
colors.cinematographic_notes: Near-total darkness as aesthetic choice, silhouettes, gothic architecture consumed by shadow

colors.name: Titanio Mecánico
colors.cultural_context: Metal bodies, spacecraft interiors, artificial intelligence, industrial dystopia
colors.cinematographic_notes: Cold metallic grays of machines, robots, and spacecraft — technology as overwhelming physical presence

monochromatic_modes.name: Claroscuro Dramático
monochromatic_modes.description: High-contrast black and white with dramatic shadows and sculptural lighting
monochromatic_modes.cinematographic_notes: Deep blacks, harsh shadows, chiaroscuro lighting, expressionist influence, sculptural faces
monochromatic_modes.historical_context: Film noir, German expressionism, high-contrast photography

monochromatic_modes.name: Monocromático Íntimo
monochromatic_modes.description: Soft, naturalistic black and white with gentle gradations and organic texture
monochromatic_modes.cinematographic_notes: Soft grays, gentle lighting, natural textures, documentary realism, emotional proximity
monochromatic_modes.historical_context: Italian neorealism, French New Wave, naturalistic photography

decision_hierarchy: Collective cultural memory of the film

secondary_colors.definition: Supporting colors that contribute to the film's palette (maximum 3)
criteria: Deliberate chromatic relationship to primary color
Film shot in black and white (not colorized)
assignment_logic: If high contrast and dramatic lighting → claroscuro_dramatico
Assignment is editorial decision based on cinematographic style

color_shift_films.description: Films that shift color palettes dramatically (e.g., Wizard of Oz, Pleasantville)
color_shift_films.handling: Assign primary color to dominant portion of runtime or most culturally memorable segment; note shift in metadata

desaturated_films.description: Color films with very low saturation approaching monochrome
desaturated_films.handling: Treat as color film if color is present and intentional; assign based on hue bias and cultural memory

multi_segment_films.description: Anthology films or films with distinct visual chapters
multi_segment_films.handling: Assign primary color to most narratively significant or culturally memorable segment
